'''David Halley''' (born 12 October 1986) is a former [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|wg}}er and {{rlp|fb}} in the 2000s and 2010s. He plays as an amateur for Bradford Dudley Hill RLFC.

He played at club level for the [[Bradford Bulls]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the [[Super League]], and the [[Keighley Cougars]] in [[2011 Championship 1|Championship 1]]. Halley won the Bradford Bulls' 2009 Player of the Year award.

==Background==
Halley was known for his pace and acceleration and was popular with the Bradford fans.

==First Team Début==
On 15 April 2007 Halley coming off the bench made a try scoring début at [[Odsal Stadium|Grattan Stadium]] against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]. The following week Halley made his full début against [[Hull F.C.]] at the [[KC Stadium]]

== 2008 ==
Halley established himself in the first team in 2008, he scored 4 tries against [[Toulouse Olympique|Toulouse]] in the [[Bradford Bulls]] record 98–6 win

==2009==
Halley was on Loan at [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], and scored both tries in the 12–6 win over Wigan Warriors, Halley played 5 games for Wakefield Trinity Wildcats until he returned to Bradford in March 2009.
By April 2009 Halley had established himself as the first-choice full back for the Bulls and in July 2009 his form was rewarded when he signed a new two-year contract, extending his stay at Bradford until the end of 2011<ref name="Bradford's Halley agrees new deal">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/bradford/8108838.stm|title=Bradford's Halley agrees new deal|publisher=BBC|date=19 June 2009|access-date=9 July 2009}}</ref>

On 30 June 2011 [[Bradford Bulls]] released Halley from the rest of his contract so that he could feature more regularly in first team rugby, he was quickly snapped up by neighbours [[Keighley Cougars]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=22715 |title=30 June 2011. |access-date=1 July 2011 |archive-date=10 September 2012 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120910173723/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=22715 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

===International level===
He has played for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] at international, and made a two try début against Papua New Guinea on 28 October at the [[Brewery Field]].

He was selected in the [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] squad to face [[England national rugby league team|England]] at the [[Keepmoat Stadium]] prior to England's departure for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Crusaders dominate Wales' squad">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7642490.stm|title=Crusaders dominate Wales' squad|publisher=BBC|date=3 October 2008|access-date=4 October 2008}}</ref>

In 2009 he announced that he was switching his International allegiance from Wales to [[England national rugby league team|England]] in an effort to represent his native country in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]]<ref name="Winger Halley in England switch">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/bradford/8124360.stm|title=Winger Halley in England switch|publisher=BBC|date=29 June 2009|access-date=9 July 2009}}</ref>

in 2017 Dave signed on for Wibsey FC, weighing in at 16st, but was still recorded as the fastest player in the club's history; just in front of Joe Brooksbank who had had 3 knee replacements at the time.
